heading: GCP or Azure Clusters Created Before November 3, 2020
body:
If you created your cluster before multi-cloud clusters were introduced
on November 3, 2020, **and** your cluster runs on Google Cloud or Azure (Microsoft Azure):

- In Atlas, go to the Clusters page for your project.

  - If it's not already displayed, select the organization that
    contains your desired project from the Organizations menu in the
    navigation bar.

  - If it's not already displayed, select your desired project
    from the Projects menu in the navigation bar.

  - In the sidebar, click Clusters under
    the Database heading.

    The [Clusters](https://cloud.mongodb.com/go?l=https%3A%2F%2Fcloud.mongodb.com%2Fv2%2F%3Cproject%3E%23%2Fclusters) page displays.

- Open the cluster builder.

- Edit the cluster.

- Add read-only nodes from your target cloud provider.

  > **Note:** If you are using legacy backups, keep one node in your current cloud provider,
  > and move the rest to your target cloud provider.

- Review and submit the changes.

- Copy the resulting comma-delimited URI (Uniform Resource Identifier) connection string.

- Replace the connection string in your application with this new
  [standard connection string](atlas-faq-connstring-standard).

  This allows your application to connect to nodes from multiple cloud
  providers.

- Restart your application.

- Make sure that your application can connect to Atlas.

- After the first change completes, reconfigure your cluster:

  - Remove all electable nodes using the original cloud provider.

  - Remove the read-only nodes for the target cloud provider.

  - Add the same number of electable nodes using the target cloud
    provider.

  > **Note:** If you are using legacy backups, wait until new backups are taken,
  > then move the remaining node to your target cloud provider.

- Review and submit the changes.

- Copy the resulting comma-delimited URI (Uniform Resource Identifier) connection string.

- Replace the URI (Uniform Resource Identifier) connection string in your application with this
  new URI (Uniform Resource Identifier) connection string.

- Restart your application.

- Make sure that your application can connect to Atlas.
